title: adenostyles × eginensis
intro: Adenostyles × eginensis, also known as the hybrid cagle's-resin fern or cagle's fern, is a rare and endangered plant species that is a hybrid of the Adenostyles alliariae and the Petasites albus. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, and is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Adenostyles × eginensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
